name: Lady Zhurong
primary_sources: Beijing opera Dragon and Phoenix Headgear

distinctive_features: warrior attire typical of the Three Kingdoms period
headgear as depicted in Beijing opera
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: warrior attire with layered armor and flowing robes

domain_specific_visual.opera_costume: traditional Beijing opera headgear and makeup

domain_specific_functional.battle_tactics: uses flying daggers and strategic traps
domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: symbol of female strength and leadership

relationships.description: Lady Zhurong is married to Meng Huo, the Nanman chieftain.
Claims descent from the Chinese fire deity Zhurong.
Mother of Huaman as depicted in Beijing opera.

contextual_attributes.origin: Nanzhong region, state of Shu Han
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents female participation in warfare and leadership
primary_sources: Beijing opera Dragon and Phoenix Headgear

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic female warrior without cultural attire
NOT a historical figure, but a fictional character from a novel
